curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.subtopic: integers and decimals (expanded form, compare, order, round)
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Read, write, and understand place value in integers and decimals; partition numbers into expanded form and recombine; use place value to compare, order, and round numbers. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

verse_ality.driving_question: How does the position of a digit change the whole number?

knowing_world.focus: Place value explains how a digit’s position determines its value in whole numbers and decimals. Students practise reading numbers, identifying digit values, and writing expanded form to build precision and confidence.
key_concepts: Place (ones, tens, hundreds, tenths, hundredths, thousandths)
Value (what a digit is worth because of its place)
Comparing and ordering using place value
activities: Warm-up: Read, say what each digit is worth, and write in expanded form (e.g., 4 307; 50 206; 809 014; 3.7; 0.406).
Digit of the Day: Place a chosen digit (e.g., 7) in different positions and compare its value (e.g., tens vs thousands; decimals like 0.73).
Quick Practice (low floor, high ceiling): expanded form for whole numbers, larger numbers, and decimals; check recombination with a partner.

human_story.focus: Place value is essential for interpreting numbers accurately in money, measurement, time, and big-number contexts. Misreading place value can cause real mistakes (prices, ingredients, doses), so precision matters.
justice_prompts: Why might it matter if a medicine dose or ingredient is written 0.5 instead of 0.05?
Which is more: 0.4 or 0.04? How would you explain without a calculator?
activities: Discussion: share real examples of misreading digits/zeros and how to prevent it.
Mini task: write a short 'number moment' story where someone misunderstands place value and someone else spots and fixes it; explain what key digits are worth.

expressive_self.focus: Students express place value understanding creatively through drawing, metaphor, or narrative, and reflect on what feels clear or confusing (zeros, decimals, big numbers).
activities: Place Value City: draw a city where each building is a place (ones house, tens tower, hundreds hotel) and digits move between places.
Metaphor/Poem: create a metaphor (bus seats, library shelves, cake layers) explaining why place matters.
Number Biography: choose a number (e.g., 3 407 or 0.508) and write where each digit lives, what it’s worth, and what changes if it moves.

designed_world.focus: Students use a drag-and-drop tool to decompose and rebuild numbers in expanded form, generate challenges, and reflect on how design features support learning and error-checking.
tools: Canva embed: Place Value Drag & Drop Web App
activities: Use the app to generate a target number and match it using value tiles in expanded form.
Build, check, and iterate using the app’s feedback (“Check my expanded form”).
Create your own challenges including at least three places and at least one zero; swap with a partner to rebuild and explain digit values.
Optional decimal challenge: represent numbers like 4.6, 2.34, 0.507 in expanded form and explain digits after the decimal point.
Design reflection: what did the app make easier, what did you notice, and what feature would help another student?
design_challenges: Design a place value challenge number with at least three different places and at least one zero, then write the correct expanded form and a digit-value explanation for someone else.

regulation_supports: Pause & check-in: notice body feelings when seeing long numbers (breath speed, thoughts).
Number breathing: breathe in for 4, out for 6 while counting quietly.
Grounding with digits: find 5 numbers in the room and say what each digit is worth in at least one of them.
Kind self-talk: choose a helpful sentence (e.g., “I can try one place at a time”).
wellbeing_prompts: Zeros/decimals can feel tricky—what strategy helps you stay steady when you feel stuck?

pathways.name: Option A: Place Value Explainer Card / Mini Poster
pathways.focus: Create a one-page guide explaining place value, expanded form, and why digit value changes with position.
products: A one-page explainer with definitions and examples
At least one worked expanded-form example
A clear explanation of why the same digit changes value in different places

pathways.name: Option B: Worked Example Set
pathways.focus: Create 4–6 examples showing standard form, expanded form, and digit values, including an optional decimal.
products: 4–6 numbers written in standard and expanded form
Value labels for at least two digits in each number

pathways.name: Option C: Partner Challenge Sheet
pathways.focus: Create a challenge sheet for a partner with full answers provided separately.
products: A set of place value challenge questions (including digit value, building largest number, expanded form)
A separate answer sheet with full working/answers

required: Recognise digits 0–9 and read whole numbers
Basic understanding of tens and ones (place concept)

builds_toward: Rounding to significant figures and decimal places
Operations with decimals (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division)
Standard form (scientific notation) later in KS3/KS4
